newsSince: On June 9 Sterling Infrastructure announced and closed the acquisition of Stone Ridge Contracting, extending its E-Infrastructure footprint across the Pacific Northwest and Texas. This follows a May 4 record Q1 (revenue +92% to ~$826M) and a raised FY26 guide (revenue $3.7-3.8B, adjusted EPS $18.40-19.05), with backlog ~$3.8B and mission-critical work (data centers/semis) >90% of E-Infrastructure signed backlog. Stock +7% since June 9.

sources.label: STRL up after raising 2026 outlook on AI-focused backlog shift (Simply Wall St)
STRL targets 25% revenue growth as data-center demand surges (Seeking Alpha)

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent lens: data-center site-prep beneficiary. June 9 Stone Ridge acquisition adds DC/civil capacity and geography, on top of raised FY26 guide and >90% mission-critical (DC/semi) backlog. Fundamental confirmation strong enough to move up vs latent peers despite the construction angle being partly discovered.
verdicts.bull: Stone Ridge expands DC site-dev capacity; backlog ~$3.8B, >90% mission-critical; FY26 guide raised.
verdicts.neutral: Construction angle being discovered but execution and backlog quality keep optionality.
verdicts.bear: Cyclical construction margins; data-center capex concentration; short-seller scrutiny.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Stone Ridge acquisition plus raised FY26 guidance on AI/DC backlog.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Data-center capex slowdown and construction-margin cyclicality.
